observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: For Municipal Accounting Office: 1.(cid:9)Immediately assign dedicated personnel to manage and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs). 2.(cid:9)Evaluate and streamline the existing accounting system to simplify the process of encoding individual inventory items. Explore options for batch processing or data import to reduce manual effort. 3.(cid:9)Provide comprehensive training to accounting staff on the proper use and importance of SLCs. For General Services Office: 4.(cid:9)Conduct a workload assessment to determine if additional personnel are needed to effectively manage inventory and property responsibilities. 5.(cid:9)Develop and implement standardized inventory management procedures, including clear guidelines for recordkeeping, tracking, monitoring, and reporting in compliance with The Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units – Volume 1, which outlines the Accounting Policies for Inventory Account. 6.(cid:9)Provide thorough training to all GSO personnel on inventory management practices and procedures. For Municipal Accounting Office and GSO: 7.(cid:9)GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office should work together that their systems and records are reconciled and up to date.

We recommended that Management: 1.(cid:9)Conduct comprehensive training for the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAGRO) staff on the proper preparation and maintenance of the Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC) and the Report of the Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA), in accordance with the Local Government Accounting Manual (LGAM) Volume I and Volume II. 2.(cid:9)Implement a schedule for regular physical inventories of all biological assets, with the Report of the Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA) being prepared by the MAGRO and submitted to the Accounting Office promptly.

We recommended that the Management take immediate action to: 1.(cid:9)Provide a suitable warehouse or secure storage facility to properly store and protect medical supplies in line with good storage practices. 2.(cid:9)Implement regular monitoring and inventory inspections to ensure proper storage conditions are maintained, mitigating the risk of supply spoilage and waste. 3.(cid:9)Ensure compliance with the DOH Supply Chain Management Service Warehouse Operations Manual to prevent financial loss and disrupt healthcare services within the community.

We recommended the Management that: 1.(cid:9)Consolidate the procurement planning and implement the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and ensure all departments contribute to a consolidated, indicative Annual Procurement Plan (APP). This will facilitate bulk purchasing and minimize piecemeal transactions. 2.(cid:9)Designate a secure and organized physical storage area for common-use office supplies and materials. This will enable effective inventory management and reduce the frequency of urgent, individual department purchases. 3.(cid:9)Establish a designate a responsible individual to manage the procurement of common-use office supplies. This will streamline the process, reduce redundant tasks, and minimize the issuance of numerous checks.

Immediately retrieve and provide to the audit team all documentation related to the Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) project, including but not limited to existing Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), letters of intent, email correspondences, and meeting minutes, to support the validity of the municipality's procurement actions. If not warranted a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Human Resource Management Office to immediately submit their existing and proposed organizational structure for inclusion in the Human Resource Development Plan. This plan clearly defines roles, qualification standards and duties and reporting lines for all functions of each plantilla positions and align staffing with the municipality’s strategic goals and service delivery objectives.

We recommended that management to address these issues: 1.(cid:9)Prioritize the immediate reconciliation of GSIS and HDMF accounts for the year 2023 and all subsequent affected years. 2.(cid:9)Strengthen internal controls over reconciliation processes. This includes establishing clear procedures, assigning dedicated personnel, and implementing regular supervisory reviews of reconciliation activities.

We recommended that the Management continue exhausting all possible action and remedies to collect the dormant receivables. Otherwise, facilitate the request for write off, if warranted, in accordance with the proper disposition of dormant accounts.

We recommended the management the following: 1.(cid:9)Strict enforcement of the submission of complete and properly accomplished Daily Time Records (DTRs) is required as a prerequisite for payroll processing. When personnel are rendering services outside their assigned office, they must attach Official Business Slips and Travel Orders to justify their absence. Non-compliance to submit duly accomplished DTRs for CY2024 will result in issuance of Notice of Disallowance. 2.(cid:9)The HRMO and Accounting Department implement a stricter verification process for payroll-related documents, ensuring that all disbursements are duly supported and properly computed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with Section 4.C.2, paragraph 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 and Item 5, paragraph of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01. Mandates per GAD Goal within the 2025-2028 time frame.

These recommendations aim to address the root causes of the delayed titling process, mitigate the associated risks, and improve the municipality's land management practices. 1.(cid:9)Prioritize titling of all acquired lands: Develop and implement a time-bound action plan to expedite the titling of acquired lands, prioritizing those with significant improvement values. Allocate sufficient resources and personnel to address each property, especially considering that the subject properties were acquired in the 1990s. 2.(cid:9)Establish a Land Titling Task Force: Create a dedicated task force composed from the MPDO and other relevant offices to streamline the titling process. Clearly define roles and responsibilities and establish regular monitoring and reporting mechanisms. 3.(cid:9)Strengthen coordination and communication: Improve communication and coordination with relevant agencies (e.g., BIR, Land Registry Authority, National Commission on Indigenous Peoples) to resolve outstanding issues. Establish clear channels of communication with landowners and other stakeholders to facilitate the titling process. 4.(cid:9)Regular monitoring and reporting: Implement regular monitoring and reporting on the progress of land titling efforts. Conduct regular assessments of the implemented measures’ effectiveness and identify areas for improvement.

We recommended that Management the following: 1.(cid:9)Require the concerned personnel to submit duly corrected DTRs with complete and authenticated entries, or an official certification from their immediate supervisor or facility head, along with any supporting documents, to justify the previously disbursed HEA. 2.(cid:9)Directed the department head to thoroughly review and validate the submitted records to ensure compliance with documentation requirements and prevent potential audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: 1.(cid:9)BAC to require interested bidders to include the mandatory DOLE – Approved Construction Safety and Health Programs (CSHPs) as a separate line item in their Program of Work and before issuing an NTP. 2.(cid:9)The Municipal Engineering Office should implement a mandatory PPE policy for all workers on the construction sites.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to: 1.(cid:9)Ensure that all construction projects under the CIP account are supported with the necessary documentation, including FPIC, CP, and Contract of Usufruct, before construction begins. 2.(cid:9)Conduct a review of all ongoing and planned construction projects in ancestral domains to ensure they are fully compliant with IPRA requirements, including securing the necessary FPIC, CP, and Contract of Usufruct. 3.(cid:9)Strengthen the internal controls and procedures in the project planning and procurement processes to guarantee full compliance with the required documentation before construction starts.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the  erroneous
recording of funds received from PhilHealth and  the  Provincial  Government
of Davao del Norte from Due to NGAs to Due to  GOCCs  and  Due  to  LGUs  as
follows:


      |Account             |Debit               |Credit              |
|Due to NGAs         |6,262,951.52        |                    |
|Due to GOCCs        |                    |1,698,293.92        |
|Due to LGUs         |                    |4,564,657.60        |
|                    |6,262,951.52        |6,262,951.52        |

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  creates  an  Inventory
Committee to conduct an inventory of road networks in  compliance  with  the
requirement of COA  Circular  No.  2015-008  and  submit  the  corresponding
reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Accounting Office abides with  the  accounting
standards  provided  for  in  the  Philippine   Public   Sector   Accounting
Standards, especially on the recognition of income under the  accrual  basis
of accounting.  Henceforth,  recognize  in  the  books  of  accounts  rental
receivables from operations of the Local Economic Enterprise.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the:

   1. Municipality issue demand letters to all defaulting NGOs/ POs;


   2. Management  take  appropriate  judicial  remedies  for  the  violation
      incurred by the NGOs/POs.


   3. Management  review  its  livelihood  assistance  program  taking  into
      consideration the following:


           a.    selection criteria of intended beneficiaries
           b.    evaluation of intended livelihood project
           c.     the  orientation   of   beneficiaries   regarding   their
                 responsibilities as recipients
           d.    reasonableness of payment scheme and amount of grant
           e.    regular monitoring of the assistance program thru required
                 submission of periodic report by focal officials/employees


   4. Municipal Mayor direct the livelihood assistance program focal  person
      and all officials concerned to immediately submit an evaluation report
      of the status of the program.  The LCE is  primarily  responsible  for
      all government resources of LGU New  Corella.   Fiscal  responsibility
      rests upon him and that includes  the  obligation  to  ascertain  that
      public funds are properly safeguarded from loss or wastage.


   5. Municipal Mayor instruct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  evaluate  the
      status of the outstanding receivables and to request for write-off  in
      accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:

      We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  directs  the  responsible
official(s) of the Municipality  to  submit  the  Memorandum  of  Agreements
(MOAs) on fund transfers to the thirty-four (34) NGOs/POs.  Henceforth,  all
MOAs covering fund transfers to NGOs/POs should be submitted to  the  Office
of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the responsible official(s)  of  the  Municipality
submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) entered into by the  Municipality
with other government agencies in CY 2019 and prior  years’  fund  transfers
with outstanding balances. Henceforth,  all  MOAs  covering  fund  transfers
from other government agencies should be submitted to the Audit Team  within
the prescribed period.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that charges against the 20% Development Fund shall  be
for projects  which  contribute  to  social  and  economic  development  and
environmental management in accordance with pertinent  provisions  of  DILG-
DBM  JMC  No.  2017-1  to  preclude  disallowances  in  audit.  Purchase  of
furniture and fixtures is appropriately charged against the General Fund.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that responsible officials of the Municipality  observe
the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 on the  appropriation  and
utilization of the 5% Calamity Fund to avoid  the  incurrence  of  irregular
expenditures.   Any   budgetary   requirements   for   personal    services,
maintenance, and other operating expenditures, and  capital  outlay  of  the
LDRRMO shall be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  enjoin  the  concerned
officials to ensure the annual preparation  of  the  required  reports,  the
conduct of inventory of roads and submission of the corresponding report  on
the physical inventory taken  to  the  Office  of  the  Auditor  within  the
prescribed period.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Disbursing  Officer  stamps   “PAID”   all
supporting documents and provide space for the number of  the  check  issued
and date of actual payment after payment is made to prevent  the  re-use  of
the same on similar transactions thereby strengthening the internal  control
system of the Municipality.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


   1.  Require  the  concerned  officials  to  immediately  submit  to   the
      Accounting Department the Acceptance and Inspection Report to  support
      the reclassification of  completed  projects  totaling  P17,701,577.64
      from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE account; and


   2. Instruct  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  closely  coordinate  with  the
      Municipal Accountant on the submission of required documents  so  that
      the directive of Section 50, Volume 1 of  the  NGAS  Manual  is  fully
      observed, which states:


            “During  the  construction  period,  agency  assets  and  public
          infrastructure shall be taken up in the books as “Construction  in
          Progress” with the appropriate asset classification. As  soon,  as
          the project is completed, the Construction in Progress for  agency
          asset is closed to the appropriate asset account.”

Recommendation:

      We recommended that:

   1. The Accounting Office thoroughly analyze the  accounts  with  abnormal
      balances that remained  unreconciled/unadjusted  over  the  years  and
      prepare necessary adjusting entries ; and


   2. The Local Chief Executive require the responsible  officials/employees
      to remit immediately the withheld  mandatory  contributions  and  loan
      amortizations to GSIS, Philhealth, and PAG-IBIG.


   3. The Local Chief Executive instruct the officials/offices concerned  to
      thoroughly review payroll preparation so that mandatory contributions,
      loan amortizations to GSIS, Philhealth, and PAG-IBIG and tax  payments
      are correctly  computed/withheld  and  promptly  remitted  within  the
      prescribed period to avoid possible forfeiture of  claims/benefits  of
      the members/employees  and  payment  of  penalties/surcharges  by  the
      Municipality and enable the government agency concerned the timely use
      of the funds due to them.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  enjoin  the  concerned
officials to update the records of actual market stall  occupants.  Further,
demand  the  payment  of  transfer  and  transient  fees  because   of   the
unauthorized transfer of lease rights under the provisions  of  the  Revenue
Code of the Municipality.

      Also, ensure the execution of lease contracts between the Municipality
      and stall occupants, containing therein, among others,  the  provisions
      for transfer of lease rights, penalty for non-payment of rentals and the  rights
      and obligations of the parties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Order authorizing a certain Officer to secure bank statements from the depository bank on a regular basis.  In the absence of bank statements, the Municipal Accountant should exert efforts to use the snap shots of the accounts in the preparation of bank reconciliation statements in compliance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the delayed submission of bank reconciliati on statements but she attributed the delay in the failure of the LBP officials to produce bank statements on time. Nevertheless , the Accounting Office was able to prepare monthly bank reconciliati on statements by utilizing the snapshot of its accounts although the same were submitted to COA only upon receipt of the monthly bank statements.

observations.recommendation: Adhere to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the time frame in which the Accountable Officers should liquidate their cash advances.  If circumstances warrant, the Local Chief Executive should cause the withholding of salaries of the defaulting Accountable Officers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004.

We recommended that Officials responsible in the preparation of annual appropriation refrain from allocating budget for repairs and maintenance of roads chargeable to the 20% development fund in compliance with DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.  Such program of expenditures should be properly charged against the general fund of the Municipality. Otherwise, transactions will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: In a letter dated November 18, 2018, the Budget Officer admitted the inclusion of MOOE for the budget allocated for 20% Development Fund. However, the same was due to the approval of 2017 and 2018 PPAs prior to the issuance of the said Joint Circular. The Municipal Planning and Development Office prepared the PPAs a year ahead every February. Thus, PPAs were submitted for review when the Circular was not yet issued. Nevertheless, the Municipality realigned the unobligated amount of P4,000,000.00 into capital expenditures through Supplemental Budgets in accordance with the Circular. All repairs and maintenance expenses were modified to rehabilitation of assets, and expenses thereto were capitalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Officials responsible in the preparation of Acceptance and Inspection Report examines the regularity of Acceptance and Inspection Report whether the same are properly signed by the authorized officials and dated upon actual receipt of deliveries. Otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: In a letter dated February 7, 2019, The Municipality acknowledged to have received the goods without indicating the date of receipt. The inspectorate team organized per Administrative Order No. 01 issued on January 16, 2016 is composed of personnel from different department with a term of 6 months. The Municipality admitted that they were not fully aware with the requirement of affixing the date of receipt and that they have signed the Inspection Report on the basis that the items were already accepted by the requisitioni ng head. More so, when materials were delivered on site, they have relied with the implementing office without inspecting the conformity of delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned Officials justify the deficiencies noted in the procurement of electrical supplies and construction materials totaling P340,739.70. Henceforth, observe proper procedures in the conduct of procurement and documentations thereof. Otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: In a letter dated February 7, 2019, the Municipality admitted the erroneous dates in the supporting documents not in conformity with the procurement process, yet denied that any irregularity occurred covering the transaction. As explained, the PR was left unnumbered since the numbering was made only upon approval of the PR. However, it remained undated until upon delivery, when the concerned personnel had inserted a new date without knowledge of the prior delivery. As explained, the purchase was made in haste as the building is about to be used. In urgency, the PO was left undated and was filled up only when payment for materials was processed. The concerned officer dated the PO on the same day that the OBR was dated. The deficiencies noted were made in honest mistake.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer, in cooperation with the Municipal Accountant, to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: In a letter dated January 16, 2019, the LDRRM Officer communicated that the same report, although not in the prescribed format, was submitted to the Office of the Civil Defense. However, no submission was made to the Office of COA. Nevertheless, the DRRM Officer assured the Audit Team to submit the monthly report for 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office exerts due effort to reconcile the Due to BIR account, including the beginning balance of P2,105,086.48. Henceforth, observe the remittance of taxes intact and within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: In a letter dated February 7, 2019, the Municipal Accountant explained that the accumulation of balances was due to withholding taxes deducted from job-orders and casuals while awaiting guidelines from the BIR.  The said withholding taxes shall be refunded to the concerned employees. Any variances in the accumulation of balance shall then be reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Office exerts effort to reconcile monthly loan deductions vis-à-vis billings from GSIS. observations.managementAction: In a letter dated February 7, 2019, the Municipal Human Resource Officer acknowledged the under-remitt ance of loans for some employees that occurs in the renewal of loans by some employees who failed to update their amortization schedule with the HRM Office, thus, the amount withheld for the month pertains only to the old loan balances. Also, the payrolls for the next month were already prepared prior to the receipt of this observation. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive calls the attention of the Engineering Office to fast track the implementation of the five projects worth P2,024,342.71. observations.managementAction: In a letter dated February 13, 2019, the Municipality replied that most of the projects were already completed. The Shallow Tube Well, Development Project, Construction of Covered Court and Construction of Multi-purpose Building were all completed as per program of works. On the other hand, the concreting of Irrigation Canal was still on-going. The delay was attributed to the change in the implementati on of project from by-contract to by-administration. The remaining unutilized fund was re-programme d to finance other priority projects. The account was already liquidated with the source agency and shall be closed upon receipt of accomplisheme nt report from the Engineering Office.
